_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 10 years of experience
clinical_indications: Fracture not radiographically healed by 6 months (FDA definition)
Fracture has not displayed progression of healing over a 3-month period

preauth.label: Clinical notes detailing earlier surgery that resulted in non-union
Radiological investigations confirming the diagnosis (X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/ Right) showing affected part.)

claim.label: Post Procedure clinical photograph of donor and recipient sites
Post procedure imaging study (X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) showing affected part)

cpd_claim.q: Does the Post-op X- ray of affected part show the bone grafting?

common_queries: Clinical notes detailing earlier surgery that resulted in non-union missing or incomplete.
Clinical photograph of affected part missing or unclear.
Radiological investigations (X-ray) confirming diagnosis missing, unlabelled, or not showing affected part.
Post-procedure clinical photograph of donor and recipient sites missing or unclear.
Post-procedure imaging study (X-ray) missing, unlabelled, or not showing bone grafting or affected part.
Detailed procedure/operative notes missing or incomplete.
Nonunion definition not met (e.g., fracture not healed by 6 months or no progression over 3 months).
Presence of infection not explicitly ruled out in evaluation of nonunion.

clinical_key_pointers: A nonunion has also been defined as a fracture not radiographically healed by 6 months (FDA) or as a fracture that has not displayed progression of healing over a 3-month period.
In evaluating a nonunion, the presence of infection must be ruled out.
The patient's clinical history, laboratory findings, and radiographic evaluation are important.
The quality and rate of fracture healing may also be affected by hypo vascularity (due to the injury or the surgical insult) and by poor stabilization.
Patient factors such as smoking, use of nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs, malnutrition, and systemic illness also contribute to the incidence of nonunion.
Autogenous bone grafting is often performed in Orthopedic surgery for a variety of conditions, including the repair of nonunion.

Bone grafts can be divided into Allografts and autografts and Cortical or cancellous bone grafts; both types of a nonunion need to be supplemented with cancellous bone graft to increase the rate of success.
Simple X-ray helps to evaluate the characteristics of a non-union: anatomic location, healing effort, bone quality, surface characteristics, status of previously implanted hardware and deformities.
additional_information.comorbidity_management: Patient factors such as smoking, use of nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs, malnutrition, and systemic illness also contribute to the incidence of nonunion.
contraindications: Presence of infection must be ruled out
